history_window.review_text:
Well built and compact. The charger base works well.

"Jeff's Wife"- These are so good! I only wish I had the original versions to compare. I've only heard negative things about the original stamping polishes from BM so I don't own any, I've only used MdU. But when BM reformulated their stamping polishes I gave them a try and I now own all the new collections so I gave these a try too. Boy am I glad I did. These DID NOT DISAPPOINT! I don't own white stamping polish, I use Sinful Colors Snow Me White that has been open for 3 days, it works but it's still not proper stamping polish. The black I use is MdU, the absolute holy grail of stamping polishes. But I have to say that these are beyond good! This black is comparable to MdU black in every way, except the smell. These have just become my favorite! I don't think I will be ordering from anyone else anymore.

"Jeff's Wife" - This is not true to color, it dries quite a bit darker, as you can see from the pic, but since I only use it for nail art I don't care but some of you might. I bought the pink back in Feb. 2015, still using it just got tired of the color, and this one seems slightly less opaque but maybe it's because that one is pink on pale skin but a second coat fixes the opacity, (only 1 coat in the pic). It may leave a few specks of the shimmer on your skin when you peel it off but they wipe away effortlessly.

If you're new to nail art and want to use this for that, I do suggest you transfer some to an empty polish bottle (cleaned with acetone or brand new doesn't matter) using a syringe with some sort of tip, an eye dropper or even a straw, this will keep the large bottle from drying out. This 4 oz bottle will last you a very long time, I still have about half the bottle I bought in Feb. 2015, and costs a fraction of the price as those other brands marketed specifically for nail art.

"Jeff's Wife" - I got these when they were an Add-On Item for $3.99, definitely worth the price. The stamper works great, I didn't even wash it first (even though you should always wash new stampers before first use), and it's slightly (I do mean slightly) softer then other clear stampers I own.

If you're new to stamping or not familiar with clear stampers, they are more delicate than opaque ones and tear easily, so be gentle with it. Wash it with dish soap & warm water, dry with a paper towel and clean it with a lint roller or tape. Never buff it or use acetone, you'll make it cloudy defeating the purpose of a clear stamper. When picking up, ROLL GENTLY over the image if you apply too much pressure it won't pick up. When applying the image to the nail press straight down (again, with GENTLE pressure) and rock side to side or roll from side to side. Don't push the tip of the nail into it, it will tear.

All the plates stamp great, I didn't swatch every image, although plates 29 & 41 have shallow etching (in my experience this is normal for BP plates) so the thinner scraper is a nice addition. It's not as thin as the 0.5mm and not a thick as the 1mm ones I own, I'd say it's 0.75mm (I didn't get out the calipers). There are different size images, a little for every nail size. But I do wish the water marble designs were bigger, I'm not a Water Marble Witch yet. Plate 52 is great for lead lighting or Sharpie water coloring.

The stamping guide works like it's supposed to, I've added a photo to show how it's used just in case it's confusing. I have a set of 4 templates from another brand already, there's not much difference in size or thickness of the metal but the designs are different. The edges aren't finished, like a plate with no backing, but I don't think it would cause anyone to slice their finger open unless you're being extremely careless.

All in all a great set.

"Jeff's Wife" - I've used the white name brand for years and some store brands but not the charcoal ones. These work just as good as the others, although I'm not sure if the charcoal really makes a difference.

The description photos show slits in the strip but I haven't seen them in mine, not a big deal but they do serve a purpose. There is quite a bit of black residue left over (there's also residue left over when using the white ones, the black is just more visible) but it rinses away easily with warm water.

I'm very happy with these.

It's gentle and cleanses well but I notice that it left a thin slippery residue that cannot be washed off easily. If you have a Clarisonic though it could help get rid of this problem. If not, maybe use a washcloth after rinsing.

If you have multiple kids or travel a lot, these covers are excellent. Well made and very cute. I personally like the dog.

Easy to clean and well made.

The description says shaving brush and that's why I bought it. But the brush box says its a Beard Brush. So, I still tried using it as a shaving brush. Big mistake. It was like using a pot & pan scrub brush. I couldn't raise up a lather it's so stiff. But it did a nice job exfoliating my face.

Badger? Don't think so. I have many real badger brushes. This appears to be horse or boar. There might be some goat mixed in, who knows for sure?

The bristles are super soft, almost like a make-up brush soft. Too soft to clean anything. They were a light gray and got lighter after first use. 6 bristles fell out during first use. I doubt these have any charcoal compared to the deep, black coloring of other brushes.

The toothpaste is an unknown brand from China. I'll try many things, but I ain't trying that.

These files are fantastic. I really like the fact that they're not etched like any other glass file. They do a great job at filing natural nails without all that ear splitting, teeth hurting, grinding noise other glass files make.

As far as buffing/polishing goes, yes, they do make a naked, natural nail shiny. But unless your nail is free of bumps, humps, divots, etc., it's going to take a long time to shine the nail evenly. What you end up doing is making thin/weak spots in the nail which will cause breaks or cracks as the nail grows out because these files take off a lot of nail. You can get shinier nails quicker with a base coat.

history_window.review_text: This vitamin C and lemon peel brightening cleanser does a great job of cleaning the skin very effectively.  Just to give you an idea, I have an oilier type T-Zone as well as dryer areas on my chin to give me a bit of a combination type skin. While I appreciate it how well this cleanser cleansed, I did find it very drying. I was in absolute need of a moisturizer, and serum after using it. I guess that wasn't that bad, considering that I always use serums and moisturizers anyways.  I certainly wouldn't be able to use this cleanser on its own, as a face wash and go type product. It really needs to be followed up with other skin care products. That being said it does cleanse extremely well, and has a very pleasant scent to it. Surely if you're somebody who has oilier skin, I think you would appreciate how well this will alleviate you of that issue either at the end of the day, or before the day gets started. If however, you are somebody who has a dryer type skin, you might be over exacerbating that issue with this cleanser. All in all, I think it depends on your skin type as to whether this is the cleanser for you. For my combination type skin, I liked it very much on my oily areas, but less so of course on the dryer spots I have around my chin.

I have to say, that my experience with Korean skin care products is really fantastic. I do have combination type skin with an oilier T zone, and drier areas that surround my chin. As such, I do find it difficult to find products that walk that fine line between exacerbating the oiliness and solving the dryness, or tamping down the oiliness and exacerbating the dryness. Hydrating products generally don't do too well for my T zone, but I need them for my dryer spots that surround my chin. This recovery cream works extremely well at hydrating the skin, and is a nice texture and consistency that saturates very well and doesn't leave the skin feeling greasy. That is key for me, because I was able to use it on all of my face, and not worry about exacerbating either issue that I have. I found that the finish that it left on my oilier t zone, was surprisingly matte, and not glossy at all. As for the dry areas around my chin, this cream did wonders. At first I thought to use it as a spot treatment cream for myself, using it only on my chin to hydrate the dry skin that I have there, but I was pleasantly surprised to see that I could use it on both my oily skin, and my dry skin. A little of this cream certainly goes a long way, as I only needed about a dime to a nickel sized amount, in order to cover my entire face. The flaking skin issues that I often have on my chin, were completely alleviated. In my experience, this is another hit for Korea's skin care industry. I found the packaging to be rather subdued, but certainly adequate enough considering the price point. That for me is another nice thing, as I found it to be fairly reasonably priced. All in all, my experience with it was tremendous, and so I would recommend it.

I wanted to try this out for the chest are and after two weeks of using it I did see no improvements at all. It’s kind of oily and it absorbs quickly into the skin. Not burning sensation at all. I’ve been taking collagen supplements for six months now and I do have to say that my nails, skin and hair have definitely improved tremendously. I usually take my collagen in the morning. I like powder  over pills because I like to add it to my coffee. So I decided to try this one which is supposed to be taken at night. I like to add it to a big glass of water. It does take a while to stir and dissolve completely. But it ends up dissolving. It doesn’t have a strong taste. I don’t taste anything at all. So that’s a positive thing for me. It doesn’t disrupt my stomach. I don’t get any aches from this at all. My skin is definitely looking a lot clearer and glowy. I don’t find much hair cut up in my comb like I used to. My nails are definitely stronger. Overall yes certain things have improved. But you have to be persistent to see results.

I live down South, in South Florida, and I'm no stranger to a nice coconut scent. This body wash smells so nice. The other thing about that, is that the scent actually hangs around for a while after you exit the shower. It leaves you smelling nice and tropical. From an aromatherapy perspective, it makes the shower experience really lovely. Couple of pumps on the old loofah, and it lathers up extremely well for full body coverage. I can't stand body washes that require half the bottle to maintain a good lather. This one is not one of those. It cleans very nicely, and does a bang up job of leaving me feeling nicely hydrated as well. So the natural moisture from my skin is not stripped away, and I'm left feeling clean and smelling wonderful. I actually lost it to my wife, who gave it a try and insisted that it was no longer mine, and that I was not allowed to touch it. Because I consider myself a gentleman, I gave in to her demands, and sadly gave her all of the wonderful shower experiences going forward from that point on. That being said, it speaks to how nice a shower experience you'll get from this body wash. I do recommend it.

Honestly I was very disappointed with this facial soap. I was hoping that it would do a good job of cleansing my skin, without drying it out. Quite frankly, it did dry it out, and I was less than impressed with the results. The other thing that sort of took me aback was the size of the bar. I was kind of expecting something a little bit more substantial, but it was more representative of the size of those mini soaps you get at a hotel. It didn't last long at all, and in some cases I didn't mind that because I didn't like using it. I honestly don't have any positive things to say about it, and I'm being nice when I give it two stars. For my purposes I found that it dried out my skin, and that just doesn't work for my oilier T zone, as well as my drier spots that I have around my chin. In some ways, I found that it exacerbated both of those problems, and I personally wouldn't ever use this soap again. I find the amount that they give you on top of things, is just very poor value. I didn't like it, and I wouldn't use it.

history_window.summary: Amazing for m extremely dry skin!
history_window.review_text: I’ve been really enjoying this face oil. I have super dry skin and it moisturizer some dry patches I have. I love that it doesn’t leave a greasy or tacky feeling. The oil absorbs quickly into the skin. I like to use it after my vitamin C serum. I also like to go in with a toner. And it mixes well with the rest of my products. I don’t get any irritation when mixing it retinol either. I like to apply directly on my face. And I think my favorite method is to mix it in with my foundation. I found that my foundation looks a bit cakey and this face oil, really gives me a nice dowy finish. Sometimes I like apply it  before bedtime but I do find that it sticks a tad to my pillowcase but every product usually does. So the most important for me is that it doesn’t break out.

I think you have to know how to use this foundation for it to work. I have a strict skin care routine and since I’ve started to really pay attention to my skin most  foundations I use look great. This is a medium coverage foundation. And you probably would need two coats to cover any dark spots and even out your skin tone. I exfoliate my face three times a week and I feel the foundation sits better on my skin when I do. The formula is light. The primer is supposed to smooth out your skin. So I mainly use it on my T zone where I have larger pores. And I apply a super glowy and dewy primer on the rest of my face. I like to apply some foundation with my finger and spread it around on my face and then I go with one of the sponges. The sponges feel a little dense. Make sure you wet them before blending the foundation. These sponges work better damp. They don’t absorb the foundation too much, which is great. The foundation blends beautifully and gives me enough coverage to cover my imperfections. I do like to set it with powder as well and it usually works great under powder. I’d like to set it because I feel like it lasts all day long then when I don’t. Make sure you hydrate your skin very well before applying this foundation. I love how it looks on my skin.

history_window.summary: Color selection sucks and no coverage at all
history_window.review_text: If you want a full coverage foundation this is not the foundation for you. This gives a more natural look. Almost like a no-makeup makeup look. They are very bouncy and blend well thick and full coverage foundation. I just find that for this formula the sponge absorbs the foundation too much. I think this foundation works well with a brush. Because the brush doesn’t absorb the product as much. For me this is not the foundation I like. I love medium to full coverage foundation and this is not it. But if you want a light weight foundation, that feels like you have nothing on, then you will like this foundation. It does give a beautiful glow to the skin. I don’t find it lasts all day. So you might need to set it with powder lately.

I really hate it when companies ruin an otherwise excellent product by a very correctable and stupid mistake. I don't want to go so far as to say that this cream has been ruined, but it is far too scented for my liking, and probably most people's tastes. The cream itself is very nice and thick, and a little goes a very long way. It is very expensive, there is no doubt about that, however when considering how much use I was able to get out of it by simply a dab with the tip of my finger being enough to spread across my whole face, I would say that this cream will last you a very long time. I have an oily T zone, as well as dry spots around my chin, and found that it did a reasonably good job of not exacerbating my oily areas, but most importantly an excellent result in hydrating my dry spots. I think I would recommend this cream to those people who have a drier skin type, as it does do a very nice job of moisturizing with its thicker consistency. The downfall of this cream is how scented it actually is. It's not that it is unpleasant, it's that it is strong and lingers for a very long time. I used it as a night cream, so by the morning it had dissipated some, but I could even still smell it then. That might be something that some people like as a feature, but for myself personally I actually prefer unscented creams. It is rare to find a cream that is so powerfully scented, that it can last all night long. Even my pillow had begun to absorb the scent. So I felt like I was unable to get away from it, even if I didn't apply the cream. I think if they dial back the scent a lot, or simply get rid of it altogether, you'll have a fantastic cream on your hands here. All in all, performance wise it works really well at moisturizing and hydrating, but they have to tone down the scent or get rid of it for this to be a top notch cream. That may be a subjective opinion, but something tells me I wouldn't be alone in thinking that.

history_window.summary: Really didn't expect it to make as big of a difference as it did
history_window.review_text: I am very religious about my skin care and surprisingly I have never used a jade roller until now. I use a derma roller, a facial stream mister machine, a24ct good vibrating and and LED light therapy. I guess I figured I didn't need ANOTHER skin care device.. But I've been proven wrong. I've been using it once a day now for over 2 weeks and they really do show results. But only the immediate and temporary lifting effect you get right after using but now I can clearly see the contours of my face much more clearly, and sharper than before. My skin now looks tighter and almost like a had a mini face tuck.  I admit that I honestly did not think a jade roller was really something that would live up to the hype about it in all the beauty and skin care blogs /magazines, but I underestimated it.  I know a lot of people put their in the fridge prior to use, but I find the stone itself is cool enough Ashdod my skin without putting it in my refrigerator.. The stone is very delicate and easily breakable and I can just imagine my Daughter or Fiancee accidentally knocking it into the kitchen floor.  I love that it has 2 sides.. A large roller and a small one for areas such as your eyes and around your mouth. I have no complaints whatsoever with this particular roller.. It shows results, glides over my skin smoothly.. The roller never skips it gets stuck, and it even came with a little diagram showing you the best direction to roll in on each part of your face for optimum results. So, if anyone has been curious about jade rollers, I recommend you give it a try. Just follow the diagram to see best results and follow the care instructions so that your roller lasts as long as possible (just includes cleaning of with a damp washcloth and doing with a terry cloth before putting it back in the box. You'll immediately notice just how much faster your skin care absorbs fully and deeper into your skin. I always use either the Sunday Riley C.E.O. Glow Face Oil, Loreal Age Perfect Face Oil during the day and either the Biossance Squallene Oil or S.R. Luna Sleeping Night Oil as my last skin care step before using the roller and the time difference is it if this world. I used to get super impatient waiting for my face oil to absorb so I could start applying makeup or finish my night routine,but the recommended 10 min a day with the roller and there's not even a trace of an oily residue in my face!  I did receive this product at a discounted price but if it ever were to break, I'd definitely buy another Jade a roller to replace it!

I washed my hair, towel dried it and then fairly thoroughly applied this dye throughout my hair. There is an unfortunate scent to it, I don't know how to describe it other than old pee. It goes away when your hair dries, but through the first two washes when your hair is wet you can smell it. I left the dye on overnight like I usually do and rinsed in the morning. I didn't see much of a conditioning or repairing effect, but it definitely got the green right. It is slowly rinsing out of my hair, but I am impressed with the brightness and longevity.

history_window.summary: matte finish, perfect tangerine shade, long-wear
history_window.review_text:
I love the color!! It is definitely a long-wear lipstick.
I love orange/tangerine shades but they are difficult to find so when I saw this shade I had to order it in hopes that it was the color I was looking for.... it was.
The color is like a tangerine color, mostly orange but with a bit of red.
The liquid lipstick dries quick to matte finish.
It is long-wear and agree that is kiss proof, peck kiss proof.
It will stay on if I am drinking something or even eating, but if wipe my mouth with a napkin it will come off. So a touch up after eating may be necessary. I don't need to use an oil or makeup remover to remove the lipstick, which is what I like.
Overall, I am really happy with this liquid lipstick. It is the perfect shade with the perfect matte finish and the right amount of long-wear!
Definitely will be re-ordering.

history_window.summary: leave on longer and it will be smoother
history_window.review_text: I bought two masks to try out. This one for dry and damaged hair and a keratin one. First, I want to say my hair is very damaged. The top of my hair seems oily but the bottom is super dry. I buy regular conditioner from just any store and they never detangle my hair. My niece told me I needed to try a "hair mask." I had never heard of it but she swears by it. So I decided to give it a try. You guys, it really works. You have to follow the directions though and put it on your hair longer than what the bottle says. The more damaged your hair is, the longer you should keep it on. I left mine on for a long while.. I cant even remember how long. I think maybe 30 minutes? Anyhow, when I washed it off, my hair actually was actually easier to comb through. I wish I could find conditioners like this though b/c I don't want to spend so much washing my hair!
I returned this. Not worth it. was not as expected. Can't get a response

history_window.summary: detangled my highlighted 15 year damaged hair
history_window.review_text: I have the worst tangled hair ever from all the highlights and dying it I've done over the last 15 years. My hair is so damaged. And also because it is so long, it's almost impossible to manager. I also have a TON of hair. When I go to the salon, they usually have two people blow drying my hair. Anyhow, I have a detanger brush, I've bought hair mask, I've used leave in conditioner, and I've used different detanglers. (I've done the it's the 10 product also). I have to say I was very impressed with this bottle. Right off when I sprayed it, I noticed it came out different. It wasn't a spray, it was more of a mist spray so it sprayed more powerfully but evenly and wide. I used it right after I showered and towel dried my hair and then sprayed it and brushed. And it worked. i was able to detangle my hair. The only thing I wish was that this bottle was bigger. It's pretty small!

history_window.summary: Not too thick and not too thin
history_window.review_text: I originally got this thinking I’ll give it as a gift. But I ended up keeping it for myself!! The moisturizer is so nice. It seemed like it was doing a good job then then the flakes came right  back so it was very surface  and didn’t penetrate. Then I’ve had very thick moisturizer  like the one you use at night. The problem with this is sometimes it made like little balls. I’m not sure how to describe it but it kind of made a layer and then peeled  off. So I needed something in between. This wa a perfect. It is actually thick but liquidy. In other words it is very moisturizing. After you put it on, you need to let it soak in and dry a little. But it works great. Normally  I have combo skin. I use deep moisturizer only in the winter.

history_window.review_text: This is my first scrunchie. I have not had my hair cut in about 9 months because of the pandemic, so my hair has gotten longer than I've worn it in years! I'm in my early 50's and it's a little below my shoulders now. My hair is thick and coarse. It's long enough to wear in a messy bun for the first time in years! The scrunchie is easy to use and holds up my thick hair pretty well. I don't have to readjust it because it's falling. You can feel the coils inside the fabric. I find it fascinating how it works. When I take it off, it shrinks up again. I like it!

I like wearing eye masks such as these. They are refreshing and energizing. I store them in my small beauty refrigerator which makes them even more enjoyable. After wearing them, I felt ready to take on the world! It comes with a little spatula so you can take them out of the jar without touching them. It felt like they were slipping as I wore them, but when I went to the bathroom to remove them, they were still in place where I left them. One thing I like about this type of eye mask is that it's easy to go about your day while wearing them. I didn't have any issues with sensitivity thankfully. My eyes are super sensitive.

I am in my early fifties. I have been using anti-aging products for the past several years. This eye cream has minimal smell to it. The consistency is a thin cream. It's not runny, it's just not thick. I have sensitive eyes and this is non-irritating. My eyes water a lot and that often leads to product getting into my eyes and causing stinging and or burning in the eye area. This cream has not bothered me at all. I haven't noticed any difference in the appearance of the skin around my eye, but because I am a regular user of similar products, I really wouldn't expect to see anything new or different. It does seem to hydrate the delicate skin area.

This perfume by Shakira has a pleasant scent. I can detect sweet and spicy notes in it. It is not very strong, which is good. It definitely fades over the course of the day, but I can still detect light whiffs at the end of the day. The bottle is attractive, but the cap or lid is way too plain. You have the sexy body shaped bottle and then there's a little clear plastic lid that has not aesthetics at all. If you could be the lid to anything and doesn't stand out at all.

I enjoy sugar scrubs! They are wonderful for smoothing rough elbows. This one is a small container. I would consider it more of a travel size or sample size. It smells good. It's got a fruity/beachy smell to it. The scrub has a coarse texture which makes it better for exfoliating, yet I didn't have any troubles with it being too rough on my skin. It is very emollient and has a waxy feel to it.  Because of the waxiness, it coats the skin. I used some on my feet and they became a bit slippery on the tile shower floor. I have had some scrubs that leave you feeling super greasy and waxy, but this one is more of a moderate level of greasiness. It definitely softens the skin.

I really like this face oil by Dose of Colors. It is fragrance free, but it has a light, pleasant smell to it. It does feel a bit oily, but that goes away after several minutes. I'm only using this at nighttime, because it's too heavy for daytime use in my opinion. It doesn't annoy my sensitive skin. It leaves my skin feeling super soft!

This night cream has a good, thick consistency. It's definitely a cream and not like a balm. It has a strong scent to it. It smells like a flowery perfume. It smells nice, but it is strong. If you are sensitive to smells, this may not be for you. I don't have any problems with it, though I do prefer citrus scents over flowers, but that's just a personal preference. It doesn't leave my skin feeling sticky or greasy. It absorbs pretty well.

I'm in my mid-fifties and I have been using creams targeted to the neck and chest area for a few years now. I have definitely seem some improvement in the texture of that skin. This bottle is very small. It's a lotion consistency and it has a light pleasant scent. It doesn't leave my hands feeling sticky, but the bottle is sticky. I have washed it at least once and it remains sticky. It absorbs quickly. I can't tell if it's doing it's job as I haven't seen any noticeable changes, but it isn't hurting anything either.

history_window.summary: Good Way to Use a Face Oil
history_window.review_text: This is a good way to use a face oil. Normally I skip facial oils because I don't like the way they feel on my face. This one is mixed with a cream, so it doesn't leave your face feeling greasy or oily after applying it. It has a pleasant scent. It isn't sticky though it does leave behind a little tackiness, but that disappears quickly. I'm not really sure what it's supposed to do. I like it. It's definitely hydrating. I do think the descriptions are pretty vague. It doesn't bother my sensitive skin, which is always a plus.

I am enjoying this face cream. I find it to be luxurious. It's nice and creamy. The glass jar is very nice. It smells wonderful. It doesn't irritate my sensitive skin. I've been using it at nighttime because it is a heavier cream. I haven't noticed any miraculous results, but I am a regular user of anti-aging products, so I like to think that this is just continuing what's already going on.

The consistency is silky with bumps of salt. Smells pure and light - not overpowering aroma at all. A luxurious hair pampering scrub that keeps my dry scalp healthier. Huge fan!

I was pleasantly surprised that they stayed in my hair so well and kept my hair securely up. I love the two different styles of rounded and squared. I actually prefer the squared. Nice and big for thick hair!

history_window.review_text:
This drill is POWERFUL!
And I mean that both in the sense of how fast and strong the drill is, as well as how long the battery pack lasts.

I have a rechargeable nail drill that I like, but it doesn't always work as fast or as long as I hope, so I was curious to see how this would compare.
Well, I was blown away.
This is like a rechargeable drill on steroids.

To be clear, there is still a wire connecting the drill to the power source/control panel.
While that cord is not a huge problem, it does handle differently than the rechargeable nail drills that are a single unit.
That being said, for me the trade off in power you get makes the two piece design well worth it.

One thing to consider before committing to this nail drill, which is AMAZING, is that as the saying goes, "With great power comes great responsibility".
Put another way, you need to be very careful when using this nail drill, particularly on the highest setting.
This drill can turn fast enough to: quickly heat up your nails, file right through acrylic and natural nail material, get away from you and damage the skin around your nail.
Basically, you need to respect the power of this drill when you are using it, particularly when you start out learning how to handle it.

Other things I really like about this drill:
- The remaining charge is digitally displayed on the battery portions, so you know exactly how much power you have let
- You select the power level with a dial; this allows you to adjust the rotation of your drill very precisely
- You get a wide range of attachments that cover almost anything you would want to do with your nail drill
- Charges with a standard USB cord, which is included (you need to provide an adapter or USB port)
- You change the nail bits by locking and unlocking them with a twist release; I find this is easier and makes for a more secure hold of the bits than the drill bits that rely on a simple push/pull mechanism
- Reverses direction quickly and easily

I definitely recommend this nail drill if you are in the market for one.
It is extremely powerful, has a broad range of speeds, allows you precision control of speed, has a long battery life, let's you know exactly how much charge is left, and comes with a great selection of accessories.

history_window.summary: Got a perfect color match with decent coverage
history_window.review_text:
I included a half make-up, half naked skin photo, taken with my phones portrait filter off, to give you some sense of how this foundation looks.
I was fortunate in that the time I spent studying the shades on the product page paid off with a pretty spot on color match for my skin, which had me very excited.

Regarding how the make-up went on, I actually found I got better results applying it with my finger than I did with the included make-up sponge.
I know using a sponge is better for your skin, but I found it just kept blending the foundation right off my face.
Whether that was a result of how I applied it, the foundation itself, and/or the sponge is up for debate.
Since you get two sponges included with the two bottles of make-up, you can experiment and decide what application method works best for you.
Side note, this is way too thick to be air brushed on and can't be diluted, so if you use an airbrush, this foundation probably isn't for you.

Regarding how the foundation looked and felt, I would say, standard.
This reminded me of pretty much every good quality foundation I have ever tried.
- It looks best after you first put it on
- It covers many, but not all marks and discoloration
- As the end of the day approaches, it starts to look "fatigued"
- It will wipe off on hands, clothing, etc... if you rub your face
- You need to put some effort into getting all traces of the foundation cleaned off at the end of the day
- For best results, you'll probably want to pair it with a spot concealer and finishing powder

Overall, this is a solid foundation that performs well, but is not exceptional.
If you get a good match to your skin tone like I did, I suspect you will be very pleased with this make-up set.
In the end, I would definitely recommend this, so long as you understand this is not the ultimate fix all your problems, find your prince, do your taxes, and make you smarter miracle in a bottle all foundations claim to be, but instead, a quality make-up that will give you good coverage.

history_window.summary: Exfoliate your skin without ruining your hands
history_window.review_text:
This is a pretty great exfoliating pad/mitt with one key feature I have not seen before.
The thing that sets this apart from similar products is that the inside is lined with terry cloth.
That small addition makes a major difference when it comes to using the mitt because you don't end up exfoliating your hand the entire time you are trying to exfoliate you body.

The external material does a pretty good job exfoliating.
It's rough, but not painful, so it should work for a wide range of skin types.
Obviously, you can also pair it with soaps and scrubs to enhance its ability to exfoliate.
Personally, I find this gentle enough to use every shower, yet textured enough to remove a lot of dead skin.

If there is one extra change I wish the makers of this mitt would consider for future iterations,
it would be to add a loop of some sort to hang it by, so it can be left to dry on a hook.
Still, I can add something like that myself or just hang it over a towel rack, so it's not a big deal.

I would definitely recommend this mitt to someone in the market for one.
It's perfect for a bath or shower, offering good exfoliation for your skin, while protecting your hand from unnecessary wear and tear.

history_window.summary: Ideal make-up when you are on the go
history_window.review_text:
This makeup is very different than any other I have used in terms of how it is stored and applied; however, I really, really like it.
If you are someone who travels or likes to touch up your foundation throughout the day, this might well be the perfect makeup for you.
It comes in a very well designed compact that holds the liquid foundation in a sponge in its own closed compartment.
The applicator sponge sits atop the cover to that compartment, so the entire thing is spill proof and well protected.

Applying the makeup I found that it blended very well using the included applicator sponge.
The coverage level is medium, evening out skin tone and obscuring imperfections, but not covering major discoloration.
Put another way, if you have a mole or blemish you really want to cover up, you would probably still want to use a spot corrector.

The foundation itself has a matte finish with a nice feel.
I've found I can wear it on its own or with a powder finish.

Color matches are always tricky, particularly online; however, I managed to get a pretty great one.
Unless I have been getting a lot of sun, my skin is pretty pale.
I'm basically one step up from ivory skin, which this Beige Color blended with extremely well.

To show how well it blends and how matte the finish is, I applied it onto one half of my inner forearm, then took photos in two different light settings.
In the photos, the bottom half of my forearm has the foundation on it, but it leaves almost no line and no shine.

As far as staying power is concerned, I would rate this is above average.
It stayed on well for me all day when I was not sweating, but would come off a little on anything that directly rubbed against my skin.
I also found this to be a gentle foundation that did not irritate my skin.

I can happily recommend this foundation compact.
I was truly pleased with both the design of the dispenser, which makes it perfectly portable, and with the performance of the makeup itself.

This cream has a very light fragrance, more botanical, than floral. The cream comes in an attractive little pot shaped jar and is made in the USA and contains imported materials. The ingredients are listed on the box, but printed in such a fashion that I couldn't read them even with my trusty magnifier.
For me this cream did not irritate, did not feel greasy, and left my skin feeling dewy. The illuminating factor I really couldn't notice. Over all a very nice facial cream.

This is a cream that is somewhat gel like, it is made in China and is clearly labeled. The ingredients are clearly labeled on the package along with cautions for exposure to sun. It is easier to have confidence in a product that provides the needed information. As I age there are more pigment areas appearing on my skin, so  I am hoping this cream will be effective. It is too early to report any significant changes or improvement in my skin, but the product does not irritate my skin.

history_window.summary: Takes some getting used to, but holds hair well
history_window.review_text:
When I first began using these I really struggled with getting them on/off, particularly when trying to put my hair up in a ponytail.
The problem was that when I was pulling my hair out through the circular clip, it would get caught on the prongs spread out along the inside
After using these a while, however, I am getting better at putting the clips in and taking them out.

One trick I figured out pretty quickly is that twisting my hair before pulling it through the circle prevents it getting caught.
The other thing I have learned is that this is excellent for putting my hair up in a bun.
That's probably my favorite use for these because I get a nice symmetrical look and a very tight hold that keeps my hair up all day long.

Overall, I am very glad I stuck with these because, the longer I have them, the more I like them.
The clips which start out as kind of stiff and hard to open, also get easier to slide open and closed with repeated use.

In the end, I cannot promise these will work for every type of hair out there.
I can, however, confirm that they hold my long, fine, straight, and very slippery hair in place well.
That's a huge deal for me since my hair is exceptionally hard to keep up with scrunchies, clips, bands, and ties.

If you like the look of these clips, I do recommend getting a set, but caution that patience and practice will be needed before you are able to make the most out of them.

I am not sure what this spray is supposed to do.
"texture spray" apparently makes your hair "gritty" according to the can.
It is also supposed to absorb oil, so I suppose it's a deshiner.
On the product page it reads:
"This weightless dry texture spray for hair provides a manageable and light hold without the crunch. It has long staying power and keeps your hairstyle in place without looking or feeling stiff"
Consequently, I thought this would be like a light hairspray, but it's really not.
It smells nice and is easy to apply, but does not hold my hair.

In the end, I feel like this is sort of a body enhancer and mattifier, so maybe more like a waterless shampoo?
Honestly, even after using this I am still not sure.
I feel like this is going to work best for anyone with oily hair who wants to absorb the oiliness to give their hair more lift.
Where I think this really falls down is in holding hair, as well as clearly and consistently describing what it's for.

history_window.summary: Perfect for combing through and applying product
history_window.review_text:
Because this comb is compact and comes with a little protective pouch I could see how you would call it a travel comb.
At the same time, I'm using this every single day since it makes an ideal shower comb.

If you have thin hair that tends to tangle, apply products like glosses, masks, deep conditioners, etc... this helps you apply those product more quickly and easily.
If you have very fine hair, this helps you detangle without doing damage.

In the end this is a great comb because it's
- Sturdy
- Compact
- Cute
- Very useful

Definitely recommend.

I really like these press on toenails for special occasions in the summer.
After you finish a pedicure, you can glue these on, then slip into your favorite open toed shoes.
I would just keep three things in mind before ordering these...

1st, sizing is tricky. Turns out toenails vary widely in shape and size.
I was able to make my set work, but one of the nails looks wonky.
If you were getting these for a hugely important event like a wedding, I might order the two sizes closest to your toes just to make certain you could get an absolutely perfect fit.

2nd, these do not last as long as press on fingernails (at least not for me).
I am lucky if I can get these through a whole day without needing to reattach one of these nails.
To be fair, I am a bit of a klutz.
That being said, I think it's more a product of how much wear and tear most feet get on an average day.
You also tend to hit your foot harder, so one stubbed toe and you're going to have an issue.
The good news is these are super easy to reattach, plus you get a few spares to replace any nails that are completely lost.

3rd, preparation is critical to getting the most out of these nails.
Getting or giving yourself a pedicure before applying these is not just about making sure your feet look their best, the cleaning and buffing is necessary to get the best bond from the glue tabs.
You do get a little file to help you with this; though honestly, most people will prefer to use their own tools.

I really like these as a way to show off shoes and feet.
Attaching them takes very little time and the end result is quite elegant.
Still, I would consider these best for special occasions, not every day.
